perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens in a Costco warehouse aisle; a woman in a brown off-the-shoulder top is bent over, repeatedly hitting her head against a metal shelf stocked with boxes, suggesting comedic exaggeration. (2) She stands up, smiles at the camera, and holds a box of 'Beef Potato Curry', pointing to the shelves and gesturing towards the product's price tag. (3) The scene transitions to a home dining setting where two women are seated at a table. One holds the product box and a silver retort pouch while the other eats the curry from a plate. (4) Close-up shots show the curry being scooped with a spoon, revealing large chunks of potato and beef mixed with thick brown sauce over white rice. (5) The two women continue to interact with the camera, displaying the pouches and reacting to the taste with various facial expressions and hand gestures.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) High-energy, upbeat electronic background music plays throughout. A female voice shouts excitedly in Mandarin, synchronized with a 'thud' sound effect as she hits her head on the shelf. (2) The primary female speaker narrates in a fast-paced, high-pitched, and enthusiastic tone, providing price and quantity details. (3) A second female voice enters during the indoor scene; the two engage in a lively dialogue about the taste, texture, and spiciness of the curry. (4) The soundscape is dominated by these vocals and the consistent BGM, with occasional clinking sounds from the spoon against the plate.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: 'COSTCO WHOLESALE' logo appears at the top. (2) Decorative Hook: '好吃到頭撞到' (So good my head hit) in bright yellow bubble letters. (3) Product Highlights: '牛肉超大塊 料多又實在' (Huge beef chunks, solid ingredients) and '最好吃咖哩' (Best curry). (4) Information Text: Prices like '$339' and '$56' appear as they are mentioned. (5) Social Proof: '社團推薦最好吃的' (Community recommended best) and '真的是遍地都贏它' (Actually others beat it everywhere) appear during the final verdict.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro (Humorous Hook at Store) -> Product Presentation -> Detailed Review and Tasting (Home) -> Verdict

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 main female creators appearing throughout the video
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Two young Asian women with long dark hair; one wears a brown off-the-shoulder top, the other wears a purple top and a grey bucket hat in the second half.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Costco warehouse aisles with high metal shelving and bulk products; a modern residential kitchen/dining area with a refrigerator and wooden table.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Bright indoor retail lighting and warm domestic lighting

audio_production_style.audioTransition: Hard cut transitions between store and home audio tracks

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: Montage: Hook -> Location Intro -> Product Feature Breakdown -> Taste Test -> Comparison
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is a lifestyle and food review intended to share a Costco product find (Beef Potato Curry) with a social media audience. It uses humor (the head-banging hook) and practical information (price per pack, preparation time) to engage viewers, ultimately providing a subjective review that balances praise for the product's value with a preference for a competitor's flavor at the end.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Direct display of the product box, discussion of price, and explicit recommendation of the item from a specific store.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Comedic physical acting at the start; high-density variety-show style text overlays.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video shifts from a promotional tone to a more critical/honest review at the very end when the creator mentions 7-11's curry is better, which increases the perceived authenticity of the content.
